source_refs: Aruism 第二章 存在の対称性 l.473-497
operators.structural_function: Identify the currently-privileged position/interpretation and generate a structurally symmetric alternative that could occupy the same position under a different viewpoint; test whether the privileged one is absolute or viewpoint-dependent (source: 善と悪は立場によって対称的に入れ替わる / 対立は相補的).

probe_contract.primary_question: What position or explanation is currently treated as primary, and what symmetric alternative could occupy the same structural position under a different viewpoint?
secondary_questions: Would the decision change if the privileged interpretation were not primary?
Are the two positions complementary rather than mutually exclusive?
forbidden_expansions: moral inversion with no structural grounding in the incident

source_refs: Aruism 存在の対等性 l.85-89, l.695-703 (構造的対等性=各存在が役割において等しく不可欠、一様性でない)
operators.structural_function: Detect whether one candidate existence/explanation is granted unjustified dominance over co-existing candidates that are equally structurally admissible; equality is structural equal-necessity, not sameness.

probe_contract.primary_question: Which candidate explanation is treated as the default, and what other candidates are compatible with the same observation?
secondary_questions: Are the alternatives excluded by evidence, or merely absent from the represented frame?
Is each candidate equally structurally admissible in its role/condition?
forbidden_expansions: asserting alternatives with no frame basis

source_refs: Aruism 第二章第四節 存在の連動性 l.543-549 (孤立せず密接に絡み合い連動し影響を与え合う)
operators.structural_function: Inspect the upstream/downstream/mutual relations that generate or maintain the observed state; detect an isolation-assumption where a relation-dependent entity is treated as standalone.

probe_contract.primary_question: What relations generate or maintain the observed state, and is the observed entity being treated as isolated when it is relation-dependent?
secondary_questions: What downstream dependency changes its meaning?
forbidden_expansions: generic systems-thinking prose with no incident basis

source_refs: Aruism 階層 l.174-207 (部分と全体), l.685-707 (条件による階層形成・再編成)
operators.structural_function: Detect entities treated as one undifferentiated level when the incident supports a different ordering/level relation, or a condition-dependent re-partition of the same existences.

probe_contract.primary_question: Which entities or processes are currently treated as belonging to one undifferentiated level, and what different ordering or level relation is supported by the incident itself?
secondary_questions: Under a changed condition or viewpoint, would the same existence be structurally re-positioned?
forbidden_expansions: hard-coding level examples such as mechanism / implementation / configuration / observation into the generic probe
importing an incident-derived distinction as if generic

source_refs: Aruism 軸 l.505-509 (対立二項を内包する新軸の創造), l.691-693 (既存構造に留まらず新しい軸・条件の設計)
Aruism l.485-487 (物事を一面的に捉えず多角的視点=axis non-totality/exclusion の source, per external-weight audit)
operators.structural_function: Identify the enclosing axis/partition that makes the current conclusion possible; inspect both alternative incident-grounded axes AND what the current axis structurally excludes/flattens (axis non-totality — no axis exhausts the total structure). Absorbs the binary-integration function of 存在の了解.
operators.audit_watch: external-weight audit noted a design smell: AXIS bundles two near-opposite moves (non-totality/exclusion vs binary-unification). Acceptable but monitor for probe incoherence in the walking experiment.

probe_contract.primary_question: What enclosing axis currently makes these observations jointly interpretable, and what does that same axis structurally exclude, flatten, or leave outside the frame?
secondary_questions: Is there another axis, grounded in the incident, under which the relation among the same existences changes?
Is a binary opposition being treated as irreconcilable when a higher encompassing axis exists (存在の了解)?
forbidden_expansions: assuming any single axis exhausts the total structure of existence

source_refs: Aruism 第二章第七節 存在の創造 l.732-739 (存在同士の共鳴による質的飛躍・新存在の生成; 根源的創造は扱えない l.729)
operators.structural_function: Inspect whether two or more existing structures/relations/tensions in the current frame can resonate/interact such that a NEW decision-relevant structure emerges, not reducible to either input alone (operational-creation / 共鳴 layer only; root-creation excluded as not handleable).

probe_contract.primary_question: Which existing structures in the current frame are treated separately, and is there a grounded relation/tension/resonance between them that could generate a new variable/distinction/relation not represented by either structure alone?
secondary_questions: Does their interaction produce a qualitative leap (a genuinely new object), rather than a mere combination?
forbidden_expansions: the abstraction/transfer lesson (discard-concrete / preserve-invariant / transfer-across-scale) — this is a TIER-C lesson, NOT the Aruism source definition of creation

source_refs: Aruism 存在の了解 l.499-541 (対立二項を統合する高次の新軸の創造 = AXIS機能; 体験を通した価値観OS更新 = 非構造)
operators.structural_function: (NOT ADMITTED) The source defines 存在の了解 AS creating a higher-order new axis that integrates an opposing binary — this structural core is already covered by OP-AXIS-001. The remaining content (experiential / self-transformative 価値観OS update, l.532-541) is not a structural runtime probe.
operators.not_compilable_remainder: experiential/self-transformative layer (体験を通して, l.532-541)
operators.note: AC-35 satisfied: no distinct source-grounded structural function beyond AXIS; binary-integration folded into OP-AXIS-001 secondary. Source itself likens it to abstraction/refactoring (l.511) — which is why the abstraction lesson belongs near AXIS/了解, NOT CREATION (see OP-CREATION-001 forbidden_expansions).
